title: Chrysanthemum cinereum
intro: Chrysanthemum cinereum, also known as the white-flowered chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, likely due to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. Chrysanthemum cinereum has been traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and teas, which are often consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make prescriptions, including dried flowers and roots, which are believed to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum cinereum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. This classification places it among other flowering plants, including daisies and sunflowers. Within the Asteraceae family, Chrysanthemum cinereum is part of the genus Chrysanthemum, a group of flowering plants native to Asia and Europe. The genus Chrysanthemum is further divided into various species, with Chrysanthemum cinereum being one of them. Chrysanthemum cinereum's classification is also sometimes included in the more general group of plants known as Compositae, which is another name for the Asteraceae family.
uses: Chrysanthemum cinereum is a lesser-known species of the Chrysanthemum genus. However, its medicinal properties are not widely documented, unlike other Chrysanthemum species such as Chrysanthemum morifolium, which is used in traditional Chinese medicine.
